findings.evidence: `TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Subcomodule/Lattice.lean:209` sets `variable {ι : Type*}` before `theorem iSup_finite`; Mathlib has the stronger `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Finiteness/Basic.lean:55: theorem fg_iSup {ι : Sort*} [Finite ι] ...`, and this file already has `iSup_toSubmodule {ι : Sort*}` at line 168.
findings.fix: State `iSup_finite` with `{ι : Sort*}` and prove it via `Submodule.fg_iSup` after `rw [iSup_toSubmodule, Module.Finite.iff_fg]`.
findings.issue: `Subcomodule.iSup_finite` restricts the finite index to `{ι : Type*}`, even though the surrounding `iSup_toSubmodule`/`map_iSup` API already supports `{ι : Sort*}`.

summary: One finite-join theorem is stated below Mathlib's natural universe generality.
